indicators.desc_en: Measures the change in prices paid by urban consumers for a basket of goods and services. The year-over-year change is the most-watched inflation metric in the US. Above 2% signals elevated inflation; the Fed targets 2% over time.
indicators.desc_zh: 衡量城市消費者購買商品和服務的價格變化。年增率是美國最受關注的通膨指標。超過 2% 代表通膨偏高，聯準會的長期通膨目標為 2%。

indicators.name_zh: 核心 CPI（不含食品與能源）
indicators.desc_en: CPI excluding volatile food and energy prices. Core CPI is the Fed's preferred short-term inflation gauge because it filters out supply-shock noise. Persistently high core CPI is a strong signal for rate hikes.
indicators.desc_zh: 剔除波動較大的食品和能源的 CPI。核心 CPI 是聯準會偏好的短期通膨指標，因為它過濾了供給衝擊的噪音。核心 CPI 持續偏高是升息的強烈訊號。

indicators.name_zh: 核心 PCE 物價指數
indicators.desc_en: The Fed's officially preferred inflation measure. PCE covers a broader range of spending than CPI and adjusts for substitution effects. The FOMC explicitly targets 2% Core PCE as its long-run goal.
indicators.desc_zh: 聯準會官方偏好的通膨衡量指標。PCE 涵蓋比 CPI 更廣泛的消費支出，且會調整替代效應。聯邦公開市場委員會明確以 2% 核心 PCE 為長期目標。

indicators.desc_en: Measures average change in prices received by domestic producers. PPI is a leading indicator for CPI—rising producer costs often get passed on to consumers. A surge in PPI often precedes consumer inflation by 1-3 months.
indicators.desc_zh: 衡量國內生產者收到的價格平均變化。PPI 是 CPI 的領先指標，生產成本上升往往會轉嫁給消費者，PPI 飆升通常比消費通膨早 1-3 個月出現。

indicators.name_zh: 密大消費者通膨預期（一年期）
indicators.desc_en: University of Michigan survey measuring consumer expectations for inflation over the next year. Anchored inflation expectations are critical for central bank credibility. A jump above 4% raises concern about a wage-price spiral.
indicators.desc_zh: 密西根大學調查消費者對未來一年通膨的預期。通膨預期是否穩定對央行公信力至關重要，若飆破 4% 將引發工資-物價螺旋上升的疑慮。

indicators.desc_en: Number of jobs added to the economy (ex-farm & household). The most market-moving monthly release. Above +150K is generally healthy; below +100K signals slowdown. The Fed watches NFP closely alongside inflation.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國經濟新增的非農就業人數，是每月最撼動市場的數據。超過 +15 萬通常為健康；低於 +10 萬則暗示放緩，聯準會高度關注此數據。

indicators.desc_en: Percentage of the labor force that is unemployed and actively seeking work. At 3.5-4%, the labor market is considered near-full employment. The Fed's dual mandate includes maximizing employment.
indicators.desc_zh: 正在積極求職的勞動力佔比。3.5-4% 附近被認為接近充分就業。聯準會的雙重任務包含最大化就業。

indicators.name_zh: 初次申請失業救濟金人數
indicators.desc_en: Weekly count of newly unemployed individuals filing for benefits. The most timely labor market indicator. A 4-week moving average above 300K often signals labor market deterioration. Markets are sensitive to sharp week-to-week moves.
indicators.desc_zh: 每週新申請失業救濟金的人數，是最即時的勞動市場指標。四週移動平均超過 30 萬通常代表勞動市場惡化，市場對每週大幅變動極為敏感。

indicators.desc_en: Percentage of working-age population that is employed or actively job-seeking. Low participation can mask real unemployment. Post-COVID, the US LFPR has recovered but remains below pre-pandemic highs due to aging demographics.
indicators.desc_zh: 工作年齡人口中就業或積極求職的比例。低參與率可能掩蓋真實失業情況。疫情後美國勞動參與率已回升，但因人口老化，仍低於疫前高點。

indicators.desc_en: Broadest measure of labor underutilization, including part-time workers who want full-time jobs and marginally attached workers. U-6 typically runs 3-5 percentage points above U-3 (headline unemployment). A narrowing U3/U6 gap indicates a tightening labor market.
indicators.desc_zh: 最廣義的勞動力未充分利用指標，包含希望全職工作卻只能兼職者及邊緣勞工。U-6 通常比 U-3（標準失業率）高 3-5 個百分點，兩者差距收窄代表勞動市場緊俏。

indicators.desc_en: Inflation-adjusted total output of the US economy. Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth constitutes a technical recession. Healthy growth is generally 2-3% annualized. This is the definitive measure of economic health.
indicators.desc_zh: 經通膨調整的美國經濟總產出。連續兩季負成長即為技術性衰退。健康的成長率約為年化 2-3%，是最終極的經濟健康指標。

indicators.desc_en: Monthly measure of consumer spending at retail stores, excluding volatile auto sales. Consumer spending drives ~70% of US GDP, so strong retail sales signal economic resilience. A sharp miss can trigger equity market selloffs.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月零售商場消費支出，剔除波動較大的汽車銷售。消費支出佔美國 GDP 約 70%，強勁的零售銷售代表經濟韌性，大幅低於預期可能引發股市賣壓。

indicators.desc_en: Measures output of US factories, mines, and utilities. Industrial production accounts for about 15% of GDP but is more volatile and leads economic turning points. A sustained decline often precedes recession.
indicators.desc_zh: 衡量美國工廠、礦業和公用事業的產出。工業生產佔 GDP 約 15%，波動較大但能領先經濟轉折點，持續下滑往往是衰退先兆。

indicators.desc_en: Total spending by US households on goods and services. PCE is broader than retail sales and is the primary component of GDP. A deceleration in PCE growth warns of economic softening even when headline unemployment stays low.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國家庭在商品和服務上的總支出，比零售銷售更廣泛，是 GDP 的主要組成部分。PCE 成長放緩是經濟趨軟的警示，即使失業率仍低。

indicators.desc_en: The interest rate at which banks lend reserve balances to each other overnight—the primary policy tool of the Federal Reserve. Rate hikes cool inflation but risk slowing growth; cuts stimulate the economy. Every basis point move ripples across all asset classes.
indicators.desc_zh: 銀行間隔夜拆借準備金的利率，是聯準會的主要政策工具。升息可抑制通膨但有壓縮成長的風險；降息可刺激經濟。每一個基點的變動都會波及所有資產類別。

indicators.desc_en: Number of new residential construction projects begun per month. Housing is a leading indicator—builders break ground based on demand expectations 6-18 months out. A drop in starts signals a cooling market well before prices adjust.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月開始動工的新住宅建設數量。房屋建設是領先指標，建商依據 6-18 個月後的需求預期動工。開工數下降是房市降溫的早期訊號，早於房價反應。

indicators.desc_en: Number of new construction permits issued monthly. Permits are issued before construction starts, making them an even more leading indicator than housing starts—often 1-2 months ahead. A permit trend closely tracks future housing supply.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月核發的新建築許可數量。許可在施工前核發，比新屋開工數更具領先性，通常早 1-2 個月反映趨勢，許可趨勢緊密追蹤未來住宅供給。

indicators.desc_en: Monthly count of completed sales of previously constructed homes (seasonally adjusted annual rate). Existing home sales account for ~85% of total home sales and reflect real-time demand. A decline signals affordability stress, usually from high mortgage rates or prices.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月已建成住宅的成交量（季調年化）。佔全美房屋銷售約 85%，反映即時需求。銷量下滑代表購房負擔壓力增大，通常源於高房貸利率或高房價。

indicators.desc_en: Average interest rate for a 30-year fixed mortgage in the US. Mortgage rates typically move with the 10-year Treasury yield plus a spread. Above 7% significantly pressures housing affordability and can freeze transaction volume.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國 30 年期固定房貸平均利率。房貸利率通常隨 10 年期美債殖利率加上利差波動，超過 7% 會顯著壓縮購房可負擔性並可能凍結成交量。

indicators.desc_en: The gold standard for tracking US home price changes across 20 major metropolitan areas using a repeat-sales methodology. Published with a 2-month lag. YoY changes above 10% signal speculative excess; consecutive monthly declines signal a correction.
indicators.desc_zh: 追蹤 20 個主要大都會區房價變化的黃金標準指標，採用重複銷售方法論。發布有約 2 個月滯後。年增率超過 10% 代表投機過熱，連續月跌代表房市修正。

indicators.desc_en: Total US manufacturing sector employment (BLS payrolls). A leading indicator for the goods-producing economy. Sustained YoY declines have historically preceded broader recessions.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國製造業總就業人數（勞工部薪資調查）。商品生產經濟的領先指標，年增率持續為負通常領先整體衰退。

indicators.desc_en: Monthly value of new orders placed for manufactured goods expected to last 3+ years (aircraft, machinery, vehicles). Highly volatile due to aircraft orders. The ex-defense, ex-aircraft 'core capital goods' measure is preferred for clean business investment trends.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月製造業新接的耐久財訂單（使用 3 年以上如飛機、機械、車輛）。因飛機訂單而波動極大。剔除國防和飛機的「核心資本財」指標更能反映乾淨的商業投資趨勢。

indicators.desc_en: Index measuring the real output of the manufacturing sector (subset of total industrial production). Captures factory floor activity in real time. A sustained decline in manufacturing production often precedes broader economic slowdowns by 2-3 quarters.
indicators.desc_zh: 衡量製造業實際產出的指數（工業生產總指數的子集）。即時捕捉工廠生產動態。製造業生產持續下滑，往往比整體經濟放緩早 2-3 季出現。

indicators.desc_en: Monthly University of Michigan survey of consumer confidence about current and future economic conditions. Acts as both a coincident and leading indicator—a sharp drop in sentiment can become self-fulfilling as consumers pull back on spending.
indicators.desc_zh: 密西根大學每月調查消費者對當前和未來經濟狀況的信心，兼具同步和領先指標功能。信心大幅下滑可能自我實現，因為消費者會縮減支出。

indicators.desc_en: The spread between 10-year and 2-year Treasury yields. An inverted curve (negative spread) has preceded every US recession since 1955 by 6-18 months. Re-steepening from inversion is often the most reliable recession imminence signal.
indicators.desc_zh: 10年期與2年期美債殖利率差。曲線倒掛（負值）自1955年以來每次都領先美國經濟衰退6-18個月。從倒掛回正陡峭化，是衰退迫近的最可靠訊號之一。

indicators.desc_en: The 10-year minus 3-month Treasury spread—the NY Fed's preferred recession indicator. Inversion has accurately predicted 8 of the last 8 US recessions. Watched more closely by economists than 10Y-2Y.
indicators.desc_zh: 10年期減3個月期美債利差，為紐約聯儲偏好的衰退指標。倒掛準確預測過去8次美國衰退中的全部8次，經濟學家比10年-2年利差更看重此指標。

indicators.desc_en: Yield on the benchmark 10-year US Treasury note. Drives mortgage rates, corporate borrowing costs, and is the global risk-free benchmark. Rising 10Y yields pressure equity valuations, especially high-duration tech stocks.
indicators.desc_zh: 10年期美國國債的基準殖利率，是房貸、企業借貸成本的指標，也是全球無風險利率基準。10年殖利率上升會壓縮股市估值，尤其衝擊長存續期的科技股。

indicators.desc_en: Yield on 2-year Treasury notes, closely tracking Fed policy expectations. The 2-year reflects what markets expect the Fed Funds rate to average over the next two years. Sharp moves often telegraph FOMC pivots before they happen.
indicators.desc_zh: 2年期美債殖利率，緊密追蹤聯準會政策預期。反映市場對未來兩年聯邦基金利率平均水準的預期，大幅波動常領先 FOMC 政策轉向。

indicators.desc_en: CBOE Volatility Index, measuring implied volatility on S&P 500 options. Below 15 = complacency; 20-30 = caution; above 40 = panic. VIX spikes correlate with sharp equity selloffs and flight-to-safety flows.
indicators.desc_zh: 芝加哥選擇權交易所波動率指數，衡量標普500選擇權的隱含波動率。低於15代表市場自滿，20-30為警戒，高於40為恐慌。VIX飆升常伴隨股市大跌與避險資金流入。

indicators.desc_en: ICE BofA US High Yield Index Option-Adjusted Spread—the premium investors demand to hold junk bonds vs. Treasuries. Below 400bps = risk-on; above 600bps = stress; above 1000bps = recession-grade panic. Leading indicator of credit cycle turns.
indicators.desc_zh: ICE BofA 美國高收益債券指數選擇權調整利差，投資人持有垃圾債相對美債所要求的風險溢酬。低於 400bps 為風險偏好，高於 600bps 為壓力，超過 1000bps 為衰退級恐慌。是信用循環轉折的領先指標。

indicators.desc_en: Fed's broad trade-weighted dollar index against major trading partners' currencies. A strong dollar pressures multinational earnings, commodity prices (negatively), and emerging market debt. A weakening dollar typically boosts US equities and EM assets.
indicators.desc_zh: 聯準會編製的貿易加權廣義美元指數，對美國主要貿易夥伴貨幣加權。強勢美元壓縮跨國企業獲利、商品價格與新興市場債務；弱勢美元通常利多美股與新興市場資產。

indicators.desc_en: West Texas Intermediate crude oil spot price per barrel. Oil prices feed into inflation, transportation costs, and consumer discretionary spending. Sustained moves above $90 typically pressure CPI; below $60 raises concerns about global demand.
indicators.desc_zh: 西德州中級原油現貨價格（每桶美元）。油價影響通膨、運輸成本與消費者可支配支出。持續高於 90 美元常推高 CPI；低於 60 美元則引發全球需求疑慮。

indicators.desc_en: Broad measure of US money supply including currency, deposits, and money market funds. M2 YoY growth correlates with inflation 12-18 months ahead. Post-2022 contraction was the first since the Great Depression, signaling tight liquidity.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國廣義貨幣供給，含現金、存款與貨幣市場基金。M2 年增率領先通膨 12-18 個月。2022 年後的萎縮是自大蕭條以來首見，反映流動性緊縮。

indicators.desc_en: Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey from BLS—total unfilled job postings. The job openings to unemployed ratio (currently watched closely by Powell) signals labor market tightness. A drop below 1.0 typically precedes wage disinflation.
indicators.desc_zh: 勞工部 JOLTS 調查的未填補職位總數。職位空缺對失業人數比率（鮑爾密切關注的指標）反映勞動市場緊俏程度，低於 1.0 通常領先工資通膨降溫。

indicators.desc_en: Average hourly earnings of all private-sector employees. YoY growth above 4% is consistent with sticky 2%+ services inflation. Fed watches wage growth as the lynchpin of services inflation dynamics.
indicators.desc_zh: 民間部門全體勞工平均時薪。年增率高於 4% 與黏性 2%+ 服務業通膨一致。聯準會將工資成長視為服務業通膨動態的關鍵指標。

indicators.desc_en: Percentage of industrial capacity actually in use. Above 80% indicates tight capacity (inflationary pressure); below 75% signals slack (deflationary). Recession averages historically run 73-75%.
indicators.desc_zh: 工業產能實際使用比例。高於 80% 代表產能吃緊（通膨壓力），低於 75% 代表閒置（通縮壓力）。歷史衰退期平均落在 73-75%。

indicators.name_zh: 芝加哥聯儲全國活動指數 CFNAI
indicators.desc_en: A weighted average of 85 monthly indicators of US national economic activity. Zero equals trend growth; the 3-month moving average below −0.7 has historically signaled the onset of recession, while readings above +0.7 (after an expansion) point to rising inflation pressure.
indicators.desc_zh: 由 85 項全美經濟活動月指標加權而成。0 代表趨勢成長；三個月移動平均低於 −0.7 歷史上預示衰退開始，高於 +0.7（在擴張期後）則暗示通膨壓力升高。

indicators.desc_en: Difference between US exports and imports of goods/services. Persistent deficits (currently ~$70B/month) pressure the dollar long-term. Sudden widening can signal domestic demand strength or import surges (often China-related).
indicators.desc_zh: 美國商品與服務出口減進口的差額。持續赤字（目前約每月 700 億美元）長期施壓美元。突然擴大可能反映內需強勁或進口激增（常與中國貿易相關）。

indicators.desc_en: BLS import price index covering all imported goods. A leading indicator for goods CPI—rising import prices typically pass through to consumer inflation within 1-3 quarters. Closely watched during trade-tension episodes.
indicators.desc_zh: 勞工部編製的全進口商品價格指數，是商品 CPI 的領先指標。進口物價上升通常 1-3 季後轉嫁至消費通膨，在貿易摩擦時期備受關注。

commentary.zh: 五月CPI達333.979，月增0.5%，延續溫和上升軌跡。過去一年指數累計上漲4.2%（自2025年5月起），顯示通膨仍高於聯準會2%目標。特別是三月至五月的加速上升值得留意——三個月內上漲3.686點，暗示通膨壓力可能在升溫。若年增率維持在4%以上，聯準會可能面臨維持較高利率或延遲降息的壓力，對債券市場和資產配置造成影響。
commentary.en: May's CPI of 333.979 marks a 0.5% monthly increase, continuing a gradual upward trend. Year-over-year, the index is up 4.2% since May 2025, well above the Fed's 2% target. The acceleration from March through May—gaining 3.686 points in three months—signals intensifying inflation pressure. If year-over-year inflation remains above 4%, the Fed may maintain higher rates longer or delay cuts, impacting bond markets and investment strategies.

ai.zh: CPI達333.979，月增0.5%顯示通膨仍具黏性，可能延後降息時程。對AI產業呈現兩面效應：持續高利率環境推升超大規模業者的資本成本，NVIDIA、Microsoft、Google等的數據中心建設融資成本上升，可能放緩GPU採購節奏；但企業為對抗人力成本上漲轉而加速AI自動化投資，維持長期AI晶片需求支撐。短期資本支出壓力與長期效率需求形成拉鋸。
ai.en: CPI at 333.979 (+0.5% MoM) signals sticky inflation, likely delaying Fed rate cuts. This creates dual pressures on AI: higher borrowing costs burden hyperscaler capex—data center construction and GPU procurement by Microsoft, Google face elevated financing expenses, potentially tempering NVIDIA order growth. Conversely, persistent wage inflation (embedded in CPI) accelerates enterprise AI adoption to automate labor, sustaining long-term chip demand. Near-term capex headwinds clash with structural automation tailwinds, yielding mixed net impact on AI infrastructure spend.

mobile.zh: CPI從320.62（5月）攀升至333.979，五個月累漲4.2%，侵蝕消費者可支配所得。智慧手機屬可延遲消費品，通膨壓力下換機週期延長，Apple與Samsung旗艦機種面臨需求疲軟。特別是食品、能源等必需支出占比提高，排擠中高階手機預算。若通膨維持高檔導致聯準會持續緊縮政策，消費信貸成本上升將進一步抑制分期購機意願，預期出貨量承壓。
mobile.en: CPI climbing from 320.62 (May) to 333.979 represents 4.2% inflation over five months, eroding discretionary spending power. Smartphones are deferrable purchases—rising prices for necessities (food, energy in CPI basket) squeeze budgets for Apple/Samsung flagships, extending replacement cycles beyond 3+ years. Premium iPhone and Galaxy S models particularly vulnerable as consumers prioritize essentials. Persistent inflation sustaining higher Fed rates also raises installment plan costs (0% financing less common), further dampening upgrade appetite. Shipment volume outlook weakens tangibly.

auto.zh: 通膨指數月增0.5%至333.979，強化聯準會維持高利率立場，直接衝擊汽車融資成本。目前車貸利率已超過7%，持續通膨將使貸款條件更嚴峻，壓抑新車銷售特別是需要貸款的中產階級買家。電動車方面，高物價環境下消費者對價格敏感度提升，Tesla等品牌的價格優勢削弱，而充電、保險等持有成本上漲減緩EV轉換率。供應鏈端，零組件與物流成本居高不下侵蝕車廠利潤率。
auto.en: CPI at 333.979 (+0.5% MoM) reinforces Fed's higher-for-longer stance, keeping auto loan rates above 7%. This directly crimps affordability—monthly payments on $40K vehicles exceed $700, deterring middle-income buyers who comprise core demand. EV adoption slows as price-sensitive consumers balk at premium pricing; Tesla's price cuts erode margins while rising electricity costs (within CPI energy component) increase ownership expenses. Supply chain sees persistent cost pressure—steel, shipping embedded in inflation metrics sustain elevated BOM costs, squeezing OEM profitability amid weakening unit sales.

tsmc.zh: CPI持續攀升至333.979反映通膨黏性，影響TSMC呈現結構分化。負面因素：客戶端如Apple、手機晶片需求因消費電子疲軟而下滑，成熟製程（28nm以上）產能利用率面臨壓力；高利率環境抑制部分客戶擴張晶片訂單意願。正面因素：AI伺服器晶片（NVIDIA H100/B200使用CoWoS先進封裝）需求剛性，3nm/5nm先進製程滿載；企業AI投資對抗通膨驅動的人力成本上漲，HPC晶圓需求強勁抵銷消費端疲弱。
tsmc.en: CPI reaching 333.979 creates bifurcated impact on TSMC. Negative: consumer electronics weakness from inflation-squeezed spending hits Apple A-series and smartphone AP orders, pressuring mature node (28nm+) utilization; persistent high rates may defer some customer tape-outs. Positive: AI datacenter chip demand proves inflation-resistant—NVIDIA's H100/B200 on CoWoS advanced packaging sustains 3nm/5nm fab loading at >95%; enterprises accelerate chip orders for AI automation to offset wage inflation embedded in CPI. Net result: leading-edge wafer revenue resilient while trailing-edge softens, maintaining blended margin but mixed volume growth.

commentary.zh: 核心CPI在2026年5月達336.121，月增0.2%，環比溫和。自2025年5月以來，累計上升2.8%，展現持續但緩步的上升軌跡。近八個月平均月增0.24%，升勢較2025年初放緩。儘管通膨未完全受控，但加速跡象不明顯，暗示聯準會可能維持觀望態度。若核心CPI持續溫和上升，支持近期利率維穩預期；若反彈加快，將重燃升息討論。市場應密切關注未來三個月數據走勢。
commentary.en: Core CPI reached 336.121 in May 2026, rising 0.2% month-over-month—a modest pace. Since May 2025, the index has climbed 2.8%, reflecting steady but decelerating gains compared to early 2025. The eight-month average monthly increase of 0.24% suggests underlying price pressures are easing. While inflation remains above target, the absence of acceleration supports the Fed's current hold stance on rates. Sustained moderation would reinforce expectations for extended policy pause; any re-acceleration could reignite tightening discussions.

ai.zh: 核心CPI月增0.2%至336.121，年化增幅約2.5%，持續偏高恐促使聯準會維持高利率。AI資本支出受衝擊有限，因超大規模業者(微軟、Google)現金流強勁且AI投資回報預期高，但GPU採購融資成本上升，中小型AI新創取得資金難度增加，可能延緩數據中心擴張速度。NVIDIA等供應商短期訂單無虞，但估值面臨利率壓力。
ai.en: Core CPI rose 0.2% to 336.121, implying ~2.5% annualized inflation that may keep Fed rates elevated. Hyperscaler AI capex (Microsoft, Google) remains resilient given strong cash flows and high AI ROI expectations, but financing costs for GPU procurement rise. Smaller AI startups face tighter capital access, potentially slowing data center buildouts. NVIDIA's near-term order book stays solid, though valuation multiples compress under sustained higher discount rates.

mobile.zh: 核心CPI連續12個月攀升至336.121，反映持續通膨壓力侵蝕消費者可支配所得。高利率環境下，非必需品支出緊縮，智慧型手機換機週期延長至3.5年以上。蘋果、三星高階機型(>$1000)需求疲軟，消費者轉向中低價位或延後購買。分期付款利率上升進一步抑制銷量，預期Q3全球出貨量年減4-6%。
mobile.en: Core CPI's 12-month climb to 336.121 erodes disposable income amid persistent inflation. Elevated interest rates suppress discretionary spending, extending smartphone replacement cycles beyond 3.5 years. Demand for Apple and Samsung premium models (>$1000) softens as consumers trade down or delay purchases. Higher installment financing rates further dampen sales, with global Q3 shipments likely down 4-6% YoY across all segments.

auto.zh: 核心CPI月增0.2%，聯準會不太可能短期降息，車貸利率維持在7-8%高位。新車月付款突破$750，壓抑購車需求，尤其影響價格敏感的中型轎車與入門EV市場。庫存天數回升至65天，經銷商被迫增加折扣。電動車方面，補貼減少疊加高融資成本，Tesla、福特EV銷量成長放緩至個位數，晶片與MCU需求對應走弱。
auto.en: Core CPI's +0.2% gain keeps Fed rates high, sustaining auto loan rates at 7-8%. Average new vehicle payments exceed $750/month, suppressing demand particularly for mid-size sedans and entry EVs. Dealer inventory climbs to 65 days, forcing incentives. For EVs, reduced subsidies plus elevated financing costs slow Tesla and Ford EV sales growth to single digits, weakening demand for semiconductor content and MCUs in volume segments.

tsmc.zh: 核心CPI持續偏高推升利率，影響客戶資本支出意願。手機與車用晶片需求疲弱拖累成熟製程(28nm-7nm)利用率降至80%以下，但AI加速器需求強勁支撐3nm/5nm先進製程維持滿載。客戶組成分化：NVIDIA、AMD訂單穩健，但蘋果A系列、高通手機晶片投片量季減8-10%。整體晶圓出貨量年增僅3%，低於原先5%預期，但先進製程溢價維持獲利率。
tsmc.en: Persistent core CPI at 336.121 elevates rates, dampening customer capex. Weak smartphone and auto chip demand pushes mature node (28nm-7nm) utilization below 80%, but robust AI accelerator orders keep 3nm/5nm advanced nodes at full capacity. Customer mix diverges: NVIDIA and AMD wafer starts remain strong, while Apple A-series and Qualcomm mobile chip volumes drop 8-10% QoQ. Overall wafer shipment growth slows to 3% YoY vs. 5% prior forecast, though advanced node pricing premiums sustain margins.

commentary.zh: 核心PCE於2026年4月達129.63，月增0.2%，延續溫和上升趨勢。自2025年5月以來累計漲幅3.84點，年化增速約3.7%，仍高於聯準會2%目標。近月增速雖逐步放緩（1月至4月平均月增0.27%），但累積漲幅表明通膨仍具粘性。若此趨勢持續，聯準會可能維持較長時間的較高利率，以進一步冷卻物價壓力。市場應留意未來數據是否確認通膨向目標靠攏。
commentary.en: Core PCE rose to 129.63 in April 2026, posting a 0.2% monthly gain and continuing its gradual upward trajectory. Since May 2025, cumulative gains of 3.84 points reflect an annualized pace near 3.7%, still above the Fed's 2% target. While monthly growth has moderated slightly in recent months, persistent price pressure suggests the Fed may maintain elevated rates longer. Market participants should monitor whether inflation trajectories confirm meaningful convergence toward the 2% objective in coming months.

ai.zh: 核心PCE從128.94升至129.63（+0.2%），12個月累計漲幅約3.1%，超出聯準會2%目標。持續通膨壓力使利率維持高檔，增加超大規模業者資本支出融資成本，可能延緩部分資料中心擴建時程。但企業仍視AI為生產力提升工具以對抗成本上漲，NVIDIA GPU需求與微軟、Google的AI基建投資維持強勁，短期資本開支動能未明顯減弱。
ai.en: Core PCE at 129.63 (+0.2% MoM, ~3.1% YoY) remains above the Fed's 2% target, sustaining elevated interest rates that raise financing costs for hyperscaler capex on data centers. This could delay marginal infrastructure projects. However, enterprises view AI as a productivity hedge against inflation, keeping NVIDIA GPU demand and Microsoft/Google AI infrastructure spending robust. Near-term capex momentum in AI remains strong despite tighter monetary conditions.

mobile.zh: 核心PCE連續12個月上升至129.63，年增約3.1%，顯示通膨黏性強勁，實質可支配所得受侵蝕。高物價環境下消費者延長換機週期，特別影響中階手機需求。蘋果與三星面臨ASP提升困難，因消費者對價格敏感度上升。利率維持高檔亦抑制分期付款意願，預期2026下半年智慧型手機出貨成長放緩，庫存調整壓力增加。
mobile.en: Core PCE rising to 129.63 (~3.1% YoY) erodes real disposable income, prompting consumers to extend smartphone replacement cycles, especially for mid-range models. Apple and Samsung face difficulty raising ASPs amid heightened price sensitivity. Sustained high interest rates dampen installment plan uptake. Expect softer smartphone shipment growth in H2 2026, with inventory correction pressures mounting as discretionary electronics spending weakens under persistent inflation.

auto.zh: 核心PCE達129.63（月增0.2%），持續高於聯準會目標，導致汽車貸款利率維持7%以上高檔。每月還款負擔加重直接抑制新車銷售，特別是價格較高的電動車型。消費者轉向二手車或延後購車，影響底特律三大與特斯拉交車量。供應鏈成本居高不下，車廠難以降價促銷。預期乘用車銷售年率持續低於1,550萬輛，MCU與車用半導體需求疲弱。
auto.en: Core PCE at 129.63 keeps auto loan rates above 7%, raising monthly payment burdens and suppressing new vehicle sales, particularly higher-priced EVs. Consumers shift to used cars or defer purchases, pressuring Detroit Three and Tesla deliveries. Elevated supply chain costs prevent price cuts. Expect light vehicle SAAR to remain below 15.5 million units, weakening demand for automotive MCUs and semiconductors as financing constraints persist.

tsmc.zh: 核心PCE升至129.63反映通膨壓力，利率高檔環境下客戶如蘋果、高通面臨消費性晶片需求轉弱，影響N3與N5產能利用率。但AI伺服器晶片（NVIDIA H100/H200、AMD MI300）需求強勁，支撐CoWoS先進封裝與N4/N3E滿載。資本支出方面，高利率增加亞利桑那新廠融資成本，但HPC客戶組合改善、ASP提升部分抵消消費端疲弱，整體晶圓需求呈現結構性分化。
tsmc.en: Core PCE at 129.63 sustains high rates, weakening consumer chip demand from Apple and Qualcomm, pressuring N3/N5 utilization. However, robust AI server chip orders (NVIDIA H100/H200, AMD MI300) keep CoWoS advanced packaging and N4/N3E nodes at full capacity. Elevated rates raise financing costs for Arizona fabs, but improved HPC customer mix and higher ASPs partially offset consumer weakness. TSMC faces structural bifurcation: soft mobile/PC wafers versus strong AI datacenter demand.

commentary.zh: 5月非農就業人數達159,001千人，環比增加172千人（+0.1%），顯示勞動市場穩健擴張。近半年數據在158,400至159,000千人間波動，均維持健康水平（>150,000）。雖單月增幅溫和，但累積趨勢向上，反映就業持續成長。聯準會將密切監測此指標與通膨動態，評估進一步降息空間。勞動市場韌性支撐消費信心，有利經濟軟著陸前景。
commentary.en: May nonfarm payrolls reached 159.0 million, rising 172,000 (+0.1%) month-over-month, signaling steady labor market expansion. Recent six-month data fluctuate between 158.4-159.0 million, comfortably above the 150,000 threshold indicating economic health. While monthly gains are modest, the upward trend reinforces resilience. The Fed will weigh this alongside inflation data before considering further rate cuts. Strong employment underpins consumer spending and supports the soft-landing narrative.

ai.zh: 5月新增17.2萬非農就業，連續兩個月回升至健康區間，顯示勞動市場穩健復甦。企業擴張信心增強將推動AI基礎建設資本支出，特別是數據中心建設與GPU採購需求。微軟、Google等超大規模業者在就業數據支撐下，更有能力維持2026年AI Capex計畫，NVIDIA H200與Blackwell晶片出貨動能延續，雲端運算擴張週期獲得基本面支撐。
ai.en: The +172K May NFP gain, rebounding into healthy territory for two consecutive months, signals robust labor market recovery. Strong employment supports corporate confidence to sustain AI infrastructure capex commitments. Hyperscalers like Microsoft and Google gain visibility to maintain 2026 datacenter buildouts, driving sustained GPU procurement. NVIDIA's H200 and Blackwell shipment momentum benefits from this macro backdrop, as enterprise AI adoption accelerates with improving business sentiment tied to solid payroll growth and economic expansion confidence.

mobile.zh: 5月非農就業增17.2萬人，但過去12個月波動明顯（曾跌至-156K），就業市場穩定性仍待確認。手機屬可選消費且換機週期已延長至3-4年，當前就業數據僅能支撐中性需求展望。Apple iPhone 17與Samsung Galaxy旗艦機銷售需仰賴創新驅動而非單純就業成長。北美市場5G滲透率已飽和，除非薪資成長同步加速否則難帶動顯著換機潮。
mobile.en: May's +172K NFP is healthy but follows volatile 12-month pattern (including -156K drops), leaving employment stability uncertain. Smartphones face extended 3-4 year replacement cycles; current payroll growth supports only baseline demand. iPhone 17 and Galaxy flagship sales require innovation pull rather than simple job-growth push. North American 5G penetration nears saturation; without concurrent wage acceleration, payroll gains alone won't trigger meaningful upgrade cycles. Consumer discretionary spending on $1000+ devices needs sustained income confidence beyond single-month employment rebounds.

auto.zh: 新增17.2萬就業優於預期，理論上支撐汽車貸款信心，但聯準會可能因此延後降息，維持5%以上利率區間。汽車貸款利率持續高企（7-8%）將抵銷就業利多，特別影響需融資的中低收入購車族。EV銷售面臨補貼退場與高利率雙重壓力，即使就業健康仍難推動電動車滲透率快速提升。傳統燃油車銷售持平，庫存週轉緩慢，整體SAAR維持1,550萬輛左右。
auto.en: The +172K May gain supports auto loan confidence theoretically, but strong employment may delay Fed rate cuts, keeping financing costs at 7-8% for auto loans. High rates offset payroll gains, particularly pressuring middle-income buyers needing financing for $40K+ vehicles. EV adoption faces dual headwinds: subsidy phase-outs and persistent high borrowing costs, limiting penetration growth despite healthy job numbers. Traditional ICE sales remain flat with sluggish inventory turnover. Overall SAAR hovers around 15.5M units; loan affordability trumps employment strength in purchase decisions.

tsmc.zh: 非農就業回升至17.2萬強化美國科技業資本支出能力，直接利好TSMC先進製程需求。AI加速器、資料中心CPU/GPU客戶（NVIDIA、AMD、Google TPU）的3nm/5nm投片量維持高檔，CoWoS先進封裝產能持續滿載。手機晶片需求雖平穩但高階化趨勢明確，Apple A18與高通旗艦SoC挹注3nm利用率。健康就業數據降低客戶砍單風險，支撐TSMC 2026年維持320-350億美元資本支出計畫。
tsmc.en: May's +172K NFP strengthens US tech sector capex capacity, directly benefiting TSMC's advanced node demand. AI accelerator and datacenter customers (NVIDIA, AMD, Google TPU) sustain high 3nm/5nm wafer starts; CoWoS advanced packaging runs at full utilization. Smartphone chip demand remains stable with premiumization driving Apple A18 and Qualcomm flagship SoC volumes on 3nm. Healthy employment reduces customer order cancellation risk, supporting TSMC's $32-35B 2026 capex plan. Robust labor market underpins hyperscaler and consumer device end-demand visibility across TSMC's customer mix.

commentary.zh: 失業率於5月維持在4.3%，近12個月內在4.1%-4.5%間波動，整體呈現溫和上升趨勢。相較2025年中旬的4.1%低點，已累計上升0.2個百分點，反映勞動市場逐漸冷卻。儘管仍接近充分就業目標，但上升軌跡值得關注。若此趨勢持續，可能減輕聯準會的就業壓力，為進一步降息創造空間。短期內勞動市場基本面仍穩健，但升息週期後續影響逐漸顯現。
commentary.en: The unemployment rate held steady at 4.3% in May, fluctuating between 4.1%-4.5% over the past year with a mild upward drift. Rising 0.2 percentage points from the mid-2025 low, the data suggests gradual labor market cooling. While still near full employment targets, the trajectory warrants monitoring. Continued increases could ease Fed pressure on the employment mandate, creating room for rate cuts. The labor market remains fundamentally sound near-term, though post-tightening effects are gradually materializing.

ai.zh: 失業率維持4.3%，連續三個月穩定且接近充分就業水平，顯示勞動市場健康。這支撐企業獲利能力與資本支出意願，有利超大規模業者持續投資資料中心與GPU採購。穩定就業環境降低聯準會激進升息壓力，維持NVIDIA、Microsoft、Google等廠商AI基礎建設擴張計畫的融資成本可預測性。企業IT預算在就業穩定下更願投入生成式AI部署。
ai.en: Unemployment holding steady at 4.3% for three consecutive months near full employment supports robust corporate profitability and sustained AI capex by hyperscalers. Stable labor market reduces Fed pressure for aggressive rate hikes, keeping financing costs predictable for data center expansion. Healthy employment underpins enterprise IT budgets for GenAI deployments, benefiting NVIDIA GPU demand and Microsoft/Google cloud infrastructure spending. No deterioration signals continued momentum in AI infrastructure investment cycle.

mobile.zh: 失業率4.3%持平，維持近一年來4.1-4.5%窄幅區間震盪，反映就業市場穩定但非強勁擴張。消費者可支配收入穩定支撐中階手機需求，但未見足以推動大規模換機潮的樂觀情緒。Apple與Samsung旗艦機銷售維持平穩，缺乏失業率明顯下降帶來的消費信心提振。整體智慧型手機出貨量持續溫和復甦格局，無明顯上行或下行催化劑。
mobile.en: Unemployment flat at 4.3%, continuing the 4.1-4.5% range over the past year, indicates stable but unspectacular labor conditions. Consumer discretionary income remains sufficient for mid-tier smartphone replacement cycles, yet lacks the confidence boost from falling unemployment to drive premium flagship upgrades. Apple and Samsung shipments likely maintain steady volumes without acceleration. The smartphone market continues gradual recovery trajectory with limited catalyst from employment data for significant upside or downside revision.

auto.zh: 失業率4.3%無變化，就業市場穩定維持汽車融資需求基本面，但未改善至足以刺激購車熱潮。消費者仍面對高車貸利率環境，4.3%失業率雖未惡化但也未降至促使信貸放寬的水平。皮卡與SUV銷售維持平穩，電動車滲透率受就業穩定支撐但缺乏加速動能。供應鏈與車用MCU需求持續正常化，整體產業維持溫和復甦但無顯著突破。
auto.en: Unemployment unchanged at 4.3% sustains baseline auto financing demand but offers no catalyst for accelerated sales. Consumers still face elevated auto loan rates; stable employment prevents deterioration yet doesn't drop enough to ease credit standards or boost confidence for big-ticket purchases. Pickup and SUV demand remains steady while EV adoption continues gradual penetration supported by job security. Automotive semiconductor and MCU content demand normalizes without significant upside from employment trends, maintaining moderate industry recovery pace.

tsmc.zh: 失業率4.3%穩定反映健康經濟環境，支撐TSMC多元客戶群的晶片需求。AI加速器訂單在企業資本支出穩健下維持強勁，3奈米與5奈米產能利用率持續高檔。智慧型手機與汽車晶片需求在就業穩定下保持基本盤。充分就業環境確保超大規模業者與蘋果等主要客戶的訂單能見度，支撐TSMC先進製程資本支出計畫與2026年產能擴張時程不變。
tsmc.en: Steady 4.3% unemployment supports healthy demand across TSMC's diversified customer base. AI accelerator orders from hyperscalers remain robust as corporate capex holds firm, keeping 3nm and 5nm utilization elevated. Smartphone and auto chip demand maintains baseline with stable employment supporting Apple and automotive customers. Full employment environment ensures order visibility for advanced node capacity, validating TSMC's capex commitments for 2026 expansion. Customer mix remains favorable with no labor market deterioration to threaten HPC or mobile processor volumes.

commentary.zh: 最新數據 22.6 萬人，較前週小幅下降 1.7%，但仍處於上升軌跡。自 5 月底以來，申請人數從 19 萬攀升至 23 萬，反映勞動市場近期承壓。雖未突破 30 萬警戒線，但四週移動平均已接近 21 萬，上升趨勢明顯。這可能預示就業增長放緩，聯準會在考慮降息時需謹慎評估勞動市場韌性。市場將密切關注後續數據，以判斷是否為季節性波動或結構性轉弱。
commentary.en: Latest reading at 226K shows modest week-over-week decline of 1.7%, but signals rising trend since late May when claims were 190K. Four-week moving average approaches 210K, indicating emerging labor market softening. While below the 300K warning threshold, the upward trajectory warrants Fed attention regarding employment durability. Policymakers must assess whether this represents seasonal volatility or genuine labor demand deterioration before implementing rate cuts. Market focus will intensify on upcoming weeks to confirm trends.

ai.zh: 初領失業金人數從23萬降至22.6萬人，但六週內從19萬攀升至當前水平，顯示勞動市場漸趨疲軟。對AI基礎設施而言影響分歧：企業AI投資（Microsoft、Google數據中心資本支出）因成本控制壓力可能放緩，但勞動市場鬆動有助降低數據中心建設人力成本，且若Fed因此降息將改善超大規模業者融資環境，利好NVIDIA GPU長期訂單能見度。
ai.en: Claims declined to 226K from 230K but remain elevated versus the 190K April low, signaling gradual labor market softening. Mixed for AI: weakening employment may pressure enterprise IT budgets and delay cloud migration projects, dampening hyperscaler capex growth. However, cooling labor costs reduce data center construction expenses, and potential Fed easing improves financing conditions for multi-billion-dollar GPU clusters from Microsoft, Google, and Meta, supporting NVIDIA's H100/H200 order pipeline through 2027.

mobile.zh: 失業救濟申請雖週減4千人至22.6萬，但較四月低點上升19%，四週均值逼近22萬關口，消費者信心轉弱。智慧型手機屬可延後消費，勞動市場惡化將直接衝擊中階機種換機需求，Apple iPhone 17與Samsung Galaxy旗艦機銷售面臨逆風。失業率若持續攀升，電信商分期付款違約率上升，運營商將減少高階機種補貼力道，壓縮出貨動能。
mobile.en: Claims at 226K, up 19% from April's 190K trough, with four-week average nearing 222K—discretionary smartphone purchases face headwinds. Rising joblessness delays replacement cycles, particularly for mid-tier Android devices; Apple's iPhone 17 launch and Samsung's Galaxy S27 risk softer-than-expected uptake. Higher carrier installment plan delinquencies prompt telecom operators to cut subsidy budgets, reducing sell-through for $800+ flagship models and pressuring September-quarter shipment forecasts for both brands.

auto.zh: 初領失業金從23萬小幅降至22.6萬人，但兩個月內累積上升18%，勞動市場降溫將抑制汽車貸款核准率。失業預期上升使消費者推遲大額購車決策，尤其衝擊需7-8萬美元融資的電動車銷售（Tesla Model Y、Ford F-150 Lightning）。次級信貸收緊加上貸款利率仍處7%高位，經銷商庫存天數攀升，迫使車廠削減Q3排產計畫並減少半導體MCU訂單。
auto.en: Claims edged down to 226K but remain 18% above April levels—auto loan approval rates tighten as lenders anticipate rising delinquencies. Job insecurity delays big-ticket vehicle purchases, particularly $70K+ EVs like Tesla Model Y and Rivian R1T; subprime borrowers face stricter underwriting despite stable prime rates near 7%. Dealer inventory days creep higher, forcing OEMs to cut Q3 production schedules and trim semiconductor MCU orders, pressuring Infineon and NXP revenue visibility.

tsmc.zh: 失業救濟人數22.6萬雖週減，但趨勢向上反映終端需求不確定性。對台積電而言短期利空：智慧型手機與汽車晶片需求疲軟將降低N7/N5成熟製程利用率，客戶如Qualcomm、NXP可能下修Q3投片量。但AI伺服器晶片（NVIDIA H200、AMD MI300）訂單因企業降本需求反而穩健，3奈米先進製程滿載支撐整體營收，資本支出維持280-320億美元規劃不變，客戶結構持續向高毛利傾斜。
tsmc.en: Claims at 226K, trending upward from 190K in late April, signal end-demand uncertainty. Near-term negative for TSMC: smartphone (Apple A18, Qualcomm 8 Gen 4) and auto chip weakness soften N7/N5 utilization rates, with customers likely trimming Q3 wafer starts. However, AI accelerator orders (NVIDIA CoWoS, Broadcom custom ASICs) remain resilient as enterprises prioritize productivity automation amid labor cost pressures. N3 advanced nodes stay fully loaded, preserving blended ASPs and supporting $28-32B capex guidance, though customer mix shifts further toward hyperscalers.

commentary.zh: 美國實質GDP在2026年1月達24152.656億美元，環比成長0.4%，延續溫和復甦軌跡。自2023年4月以來累計成長7.0%，年化增速約1.8%，略低於健康區間2-3%。近季度成長有所放緩，2025年第四季環比僅0.3%，顯示經濟動能減弱跡象。儘管未現衰退風險，但增速低迷可能促使聯準會維持寬鬆政策立場，市場需密切關注後續就業與通膨數據。
commentary.en: US real GDP reached $24,152.66 billion in Q1 2026, growing 0.4% QoQ, maintaining a modest recovery pace. Since April 2023, cumulative growth stands at 7.0%, translating to approximately 1.8% annualized—below the healthy 2-3% range. Recent quarterly momentum has softened, with Q4 2025 showing just 0.3% growth, signaling potential economic deceleration. No recession risk emerges from consecutive negative quarters, but subdued growth may encourage the Fed to sustain accommodative policy. Watch employment and inflation data closely.

ai.zh: GDP達24,152.7億美元，季增0.4%，顯示經濟穩健擴張。儘管增速放緩至略低於2%年化水平，企業數位轉型與生產力提升需求持續支撐AI基礎建設投資。微軟、Google等超大規模業者的資本支出維持強勁，GPU需求保持高位。然而經濟減速若持續可能促使部分企業延後非關鍵AI專案，但核心雲端運算與推理工作負載仍具韌性。
ai.en: GDP reached $24,152.7B with +0.4% QoQ growth, translating to sub-2% annualized pace. While economic momentum moderates, enterprise AI infrastructure spending remains robust as hyperscalers prioritize productivity gains and competitive positioning. NVIDIA GPU demand stays elevated for training and inference workloads. However, decelerating growth may prompt CFOs to scrutinize discretionary AI projects more carefully. Data center capex by Microsoft, Google, and AWS continues unabated, supporting semiconductor demand despite broader macro headwinds.

mobile.zh: GDP季增0.4%反映消費動能溫和，對智慧型手機需求構成壓力。近兩季GDP從24,026億微幅上升至24,152億，增長趨緩暗示消費者支出謹慎。高階iPhone與旗艦Android機型面臨換機週期延長風險，尤其在利率維持高位環境下。蘋果與三星出貨量預計持平或小幅下滑，中階機型市佔可能上升，平均售價承壓，對零組件需求組合產生結構性影響。
mobile.en: GDP's modest +0.4% QoQ gain ($96.9B increase to $24,152.7B) signals tepid consumer spending momentum. The slowdown from prior quarters suggests extended replacement cycles, particularly for premium devices. Apple and Samsung face headwinds as consumers defer $1,000+ flagship purchases amid persistent inflation. Shipment mix likely shifts toward mid-tier models, compressing ASPs and component content. Promotional intensity may rise in 2H 2026 to stimulate demand, pressuring margins across the smartphone value chain despite stable employment levels.

auto.zh: GDP增至24,152.7億但季增僅0.4%，顯著低於健康的2-3%水平，對汽車銷售構成逆風。經濟成長減速加上融資成本仍高，消費者購車意願轉趨保守，新車銷售年率預計維持1,550萬輛左右而非擴張至1,600萬輛以上。電動車採用率成長放緩，Tesla與傳統車廠面臨庫存壓力。然供應鏈正常化使交車時程改善，商用車需求因企業投資謹慎而疲軟，半導體內容成長受限。
auto.en: GDP grew just 0.4% QoQ to $24,152.7B, well below the healthy 2-3% benchmark, creating automotive headwinds. Slowing economic momentum combined with elevated financing costs (despite stable rates) constrains SAAR to ~15.5M units rather than expansion toward 16M+. EV adoption rate growth decelerates as early adopters saturate and price-sensitive buyers hesitate. Tesla and legacy OEMs face inventory accumulation risks. Fleet and commercial vehicle demand weakens as corporate capex turns cautious. MCU and ADAS content growth stalls despite improving supply chain normalization.

tsmc.zh: GDP達24,152.7億、季增0.4%，對台積電影響呈現分化。AI加速器與HPC需求持續推動3nm/5nm先進製程滿載，蘋果A系列與M系列處理器訂單穩定。然而整體經濟減速使智慧型手機與PC相關的7nm及以上成熟製程利用率下滑至75-80%。客戶組合中，NVIDIA/AMD訂單強勁抵銷消費電子疲軟，但資本支出展望轉趨謹慎，2026下半年擴產計畫可能微調時程以配合需求能見度降低。
tsmc.en: GDP's +0.4% QoQ growth to $24,152.7B creates divergent impacts for TSMC. AI/HPC demand keeps 3nm/5nm advanced nodes at >90% utilization, driven by NVIDIA H100/B200 and AMD MI300 orders. Apple's processor volumes remain stable despite iPhone softness. However, broader economic deceleration pressures 7nm+ mature nodes to 75-80% utilization as smartphone and PC chip demand weakens. Customer mix tilts toward high-margin AI accelerators offsetting consumer electronics headwinds. Capex outlook turns cautious; 2nm ramp timing may adjust based on reduced demand visibility in 2H 2026.

commentary.zh: 5月零售銷售（不含汽車）達662,752百萬美元，月增1.0%，顯示消費動能持續穩健。自6月以來累計成長6.2%，且近三月加速上升（3月以來月均增長約1.3%），反映美國家庭支出韌性。消費支出佔GDP七成，此強勢表現暗示經濟基本面穩定，可能支持聯邦準備局維持謹慎態度，市場應關注通膨是否隨消費回溫而升溫，以及後續政策調整空間。
commentary.en: May ex-auto retail sales reached $662.8B, up 1.0% month-over-month, extending a six-month rally (+6.2% since June). Recent momentum accelerated notably—last three months averaged ~1.3% monthly gains versus earlier slower growth. This sustained consumer strength underpins economic resilience and suggests robust demand conditions. For policy, persistent retail vigor may constrain Fed rate cuts if inflation pressures reemerge. Markets should monitor whether consumption acceleration translates to price pressures, potentially affecting the inflation-growth calculus.

ai.zh: 零售銷售額達 6,628 億美元，月增 1.0%，顯示消費者支出強勁，企業營收成長推動雲端服務需求。電商與數位交易量增加直接刺激超大規模業者（Microsoft Azure、Google Cloud）擴充資料中心容量，帶動 NVIDIA GPU 與 AI 訓練晶片需求。零售數據分析與個人化推薦系統的 AI 投資持續加速，支撐 AI 資本支出展望。
ai.en: Retail sales at $662.8B (+1.0% MoM) signal robust consumer spending, driving enterprise revenue growth and cloud service demand. Strong e-commerce activity compels hyperscalers (Microsoft, Google) to expand data center capacity, directly boosting NVIDIA GPU orders and AI training chip demand. Retailers increase AI investments in predictive analytics and personalization engines, sustaining AI capex momentum across the supply chain.

mobile.zh: 零售銷售連續兩月加速成長（3 月 +1.9%、5 月 +1.0%），反映可支配所得改善，有利於高單價智慧手機換機需求。Apple iPhone 與 Samsung 旗艦機在零售通路銷售回溫，尤其 5 月數據顯示消費信心恢復。消費電子產品於零售場景的強勁表現，推動品牌廠加大 Q3 備貨力度，利好手機供應鏈訂單能見度。
mobile.en: Accelerating retail sales in recent months (March +1.9%, May +1.0%) reflect improved disposable income, supporting premium smartphone replacement cycles. Apple iPhone and Samsung flagship sales rebound at retail channels as May data confirms recovering consumer confidence. Strong consumer electronics performance drives brand vendors to increase Q3 inventory builds, enhancing supply chain order visibility and component demand for mobile processors and displays.

auto.zh: 本指標剔除汽車銷售，但零售強勁（+1.0%）暗示整體消費韌性可能外溢至車市。然而從 10 月的 6,313 億回落後才於近期恢復，顯示消費復甦並不均衡。若消費者優先支出集中於非耐久財，加上潛在利率壓力使汽車貸款成本居高不下，EV 滲透率提升速度可能受限，傳統與電動車廠須謹慎評估訂單前景。
auto.en: While auto sales are excluded, the +1.0% retail gain suggests broader consumer resilience that may spill over to vehicle demand. However, the October dip to $631.3B and uneven recovery indicate fragmented spending patterns. If consumers prioritize non-durable goods and elevated auto loan rates (from persistent monetary tightness) suppress financing appetite, EV adoption pace faces headwinds. Both legacy and electric automakers should temper production forecasts pending financing cost trends.

tsmc.zh: 零售銷售月增 1.0% 至 6,628 億美元，消費電子與雲端需求雙向成長，直接提升 TSMC 先進製程（3nm/5nm）晶圓投片需求。手機品牌廠備貨與 AI 加速器訂單同步增加，客戶組合中 Apple A 系列、NVIDIA H100/B200 晶片拉貨強勁。強勁零售數據支撐終端需求能見度，鞏固 TSMC 2026 下半年產能利用率與資本支出信心，先進封裝 CoWoS 訂單滿載。
tsmc.en: Retail sales rising 1.0% to $662.8B drive dual demand from consumer electronics and cloud infrastructure, directly boosting TSMC's advanced node (3nm/5nm) wafer starts. Smartphone inventory builds and AI accelerator orders converge, with Apple A-series and NVIDIA H100/B200 chips driving strong pull-in. Robust retail data strengthens end-demand visibility, reinforcing TSMC's 2H26 capacity utilization and capex confidence. CoWoS advanced packaging lines remain fully loaded amid AI chip surge.

commentary.zh: 聯邦基金利率在5月份微幅下降至3.63%，延續自去年6月以來的持續降息趨勢。自2025年6月的4.33%高點以來，已累計下降70個基點。近四個月（1月至4月）利率維持在3.64%的平台，本月僅小幅調整1個基點，顯示聯準會政策已趨於穩定。此輪降息週期反映通膨壓力逐漸緩解，聯準會從緊縮轉向寬鬆立場。未來政策方向將取決於通膨數據與就業市場表現，市場應密切關注是否已達到降息週期的終點。
commentary.en: The Federal Funds Rate declined modestly to 3.63% in May, continuing the easing cycle that began in June 2025 from a peak of 4.33%. Cumulative cuts total 70 basis points since that high point. The rate has held steady at 3.64% for four consecutive months (January-April), with only a 1 bp adjustment this month, signaling policy stabilization. This easing reflects diminishing inflation pressures as the Fed shifted from tightening. Future direction hinges on inflation trends and labor market dynamics—markets should watch for signals indicating the cycle's endpoint.

ai.zh: 聯邦基金利率自2025年6月的4.33%降至2026年5月的3.63%，累積降幅70個基點，顯著降低超大規模業者的債務融資成本。微軟、Google等廠商數十億美元資料中心建設的資本成本下降，加速AI基礎建設投資。NVIDIA等供應商的GPU訂單能見度改善，惟利率已連續四個月穩定在3.64%附近，降息週期進入觀察期，後續刺激力道將趨緩。
ai.en: The 70-basis-point decline from 4.33% (June 2025) to 3.63% (May 2026) materially reduces debt servicing costs for hyperscaler capex programs. Microsoft, Google, and AWS benefit from cheaper financing for multi-billion-dollar data center buildouts, sustaining NVIDIA GPU orders and accelerating AI infrastructure deployment. However, the rate has plateaued at ~3.64% for four consecutive months, signaling the easing cycle may be pausing, tempering further near-term stimulus for capital-intensive AI investments.

mobile.zh: 利率降至3.63%理論上可降低消費者分期付款成本，但降息自2025年11月起已放緩，近四個月持平於3.64%，對終端需求刺激有限。智慧型手機市場已進入成熟期，換機週期延長至3-4年，即使融資成本略降，蘋果、三星出貨量增長仍受制於創新不足。高階機種需求略有支撐，但中低階市場對利率敏感度已鈍化，整體效益中性偏弱。
mobile.en: The drop to 3.63% marginally lowers installment financing costs for consumers, but the plateau since February 2026 limits incremental demand stimulus. Smartphone replacement cycles remain extended at 3-4 years despite easier credit; Apple and Samsung face saturation with minimal unit growth. Premium flagship demand shows slight support from cheaper financing, yet mid-to-low tier segments exhibit declining rate sensitivity. The 0.01% May dip is negligible—overall impact remains muted given structural market maturity.

auto.zh: 利率從4.33%降至3.63%直接壓低汽車貸款成本，五年期車貸利率跟隨下行約60-70個基點，顯著改善購車負擔能力。電動車售價較高對融資成本更敏感，降息有助特斯拉、福特等品牌的EV銷售轉換率。皮卡與SUV等高單價車款融資需求回升，帶動車用半導體與MCU內容需求。惟利率已連四個月穩定，後續刺激需看Fed是否重啟降息。
auto.en: The 70-bp decline from 4.33% to 3.63% translates into 60-70 bp lower auto loan rates, meaningfully improving affordability for truck and SUV buyers. EVs, with higher sticker prices, benefit disproportionately—Tesla and legacy OEMs see improved financing economics driving conversion. Pickup and large vehicle demand recovery boosts semiconductor content per unit (MCU, power management). However, the four-month stall at ~3.64% suggests financing relief has plateaued; further sales momentum depends on Fed resuming cuts amid stable economic data.

tsmc.zh: 降息週期降低超大規模客戶的資本支出門檻，微軟、Google加碼AI晶片投資直接提升台積電3nm及CoWoS先進封裝需求。汽車市場回暖帶動車用MCU晶圓投片量，惟智慧型手機需求平淡限縮5nm以下手機晶片產能利用率。整體而言，AI相關先進製程訂單成長抵銷消費電子疲弱，資本支出展望維持穩健，3.63%利率環境有利客戶擴大下單。
tsmc.en: Lower rates at 3.63% reduce financing costs for hyperscaler capex, driving robust AI chip demand—Microsoft and Google accelerate 3nm and CoWoS advanced packaging orders at TSMC. Automotive recovery from cheaper loans boosts MCU wafer starts. Yet smartphone stagnation caps 5nm/4nm utilization for mobile SoCs. Net effect is positive: AI server chips and HPC offset consumer electronics weakness, supporting TSMC's advanced node loading and sustaining capex outlook. The four-month rate stability provides predictable planning for customer order commitments through 2026.

commentary.zh: 30年期房貸利率報6.47%，較前週微幅下降0.05個百分點。近六週波動於6.23%-6.53%區間，整體呈現高位震盪態勢。雖未突破7%心理關卡，但持續高於6.3%水準仍對購房可負擔性構成壓力。利率走勢反映市場對聯準會政策路徑的預期調整，以及10年期美債殖利率的波動。購房需求受抑制風險依然存在，後續應關注通膨數據與聯準會立場對房貸利率的影響。
commentary.en: The 30-year mortgage rate eased to 6.47%, down 0.05% week-over-week. Recent volatility between 6.23%-6.53% shows persistent elevation, keeping affordability pressure intact despite staying below the critical 7% threshold. The six-week sideways movement reflects market reassessment of Fed policy expectations and 10-year Treasury yield fluctuations. Housing demand remains vulnerable to rate stickiness. Monitor upcoming inflation data and Fed communications for signals on future mortgage rate direction.

ai.zh: 30年房貸利率6.47%並微降0.05個百分點，對AI產業影響有限。數據中心及超大規模業者的資本支出主要受企業融資成本（投資級債券利率）影響，而非住宅房貸。儘管房貸利率自4月低點6.23%反彈，仍低於7%抑制消費門檻，不致衝擊雲端服務需求。NVIDIA、微軟等AI Capex決策更依賴算力競爭與AI營收預期，短期住房負擔能力變化對GPU訂單無直質影響。
ai.en: At 6.47% (-0.05pp), mortgage rates remain largely irrelevant to AI capex cycles. Hyperscalers' data center investments depend on corporate bond yields and equity financing, not residential mortgage costs. The recent uptick from April's 6.23% low stays well below the 7% affordability crisis threshold that might dampen cloud service consumption. NVIDIA and Microsoft GPU procurement decisions are driven by compute race dynamics and AI revenue forecasts, insulated from near-term housing market fluctuations affecting consumer balance sheets.

mobile.zh: 房貸利率6.47%較前週下降0.05個百分點，但自4月低點已累升0.24個百分點，對手機需求構成溫和逆風。利率上升侵蝕可支配所得，延緩換機週期，尤其影響iPhone等高價機種。然智慧型手機屬非耐久財，對利率敏感度低於汽車或房屋。當前水準未達7%心理關卡，消費凍結效應有限。蘋果、三星出貨量更受AI功能差異化及存量機齡影響，利率僅為次要變數。
mobile.en: The 6.47% rate (-0.05pp week-over-week but +0.24pp from April lows) poses mild headwinds to smartphone demand via reduced discretionary income. Higher mortgage obligations can delay iPhone and Galaxy upgrade cycles, particularly for premium SKUs. However, smartphones are non-durable purchases with lower rate sensitivity than autos or homes. At sub-7% levels, freezing effects remain modest. Apple and Samsung shipment trends hinge more on AI feature differentiation and device age distribution than marginal mortgage cost changes in the 6.2-6.5% band.

auto.zh: 房貸利率6.47%雖週減0.05個百分點，但5-6月區間波動於6.3-6.53%高位，持續擠壓購車能力。房貸與汽車貸款利率高度連動，當前房貸成本加重家庭債務負擔，直接削弱購車頭期款與月供能力。皮卡及電動車等高單價車款受創較深，延緩EV普及速度。MCU、車用半導體內容需求隨新車銷量疲軟而承壓。利率需持續降至6%以下才能實質激勵融資型購車需求並改善供應鏈稼動率。
auto.en: At 6.47% (-0.05pp), mortgage rates remain elevated in the 6.3-6.53% May-June range, squeezing auto affordability. High mortgage burdens reduce down payment capacity and monthly budget for car loans, which track mortgage rate trends closely. Pickup trucks and EVs face steeper demand softness due to higher transaction prices, slowing electrification. This pressures MCU and automotive semiconductor content per vehicle as OEMs cut production. Auto loan financing won't meaningfully recover until rates sustain below 6%, improving supply chain utilization and inventory turnover.

tsmc.zh: 房貸利率6.47%對台積電影響偏中性且間接。利率微降有助穩定消費性電子需求（手機、PC的N3/N5晶片），但5-6月利率反彈抵銷部分效益。AI及數據中心客戶（NVIDIA H100/B200使用CoWoS先進封裝）不受房貸波動影響，持續驅動N3E/N2產能滿載。汽車利率敏感度較高，N7/N5車用MCU需求可能溫和放緩。整體而言，先進製程需求由企業AI資本支出主導，6.47%利率水準未改變客戶組合或資本支出藍圖。
tsmc.en: At 6.47% (-0.05pp), mortgage rates have neutral-to-modest impact on TSMC. The slight decline supports consumer device demand for N3/N5 chips in smartphones and PCs, though May-June volatility in the 6.3-6.5% band tempers benefits. AI and datacenter customers (NVIDIA's CoWoS-enabled H100/B200) remain insulated, sustaining N3E/N2 utilization at full capacity. Automotive is more rate-sensitive; N7/N5 MCU wafer demand may soften marginally. Overall, leading-edge node demand is driven by enterprise AI capex, not residential mortgage costs—current levels don't alter TSMC's customer mix or advanced packaging roadmap.

commentary.zh: 美國製造業就業於5月止於12,605千人，月增7千人（+0.1%），扭轉先前連續6個月下滑的趨勢。自2025年6月高點12,636千人以來，累計流失31千人（-0.2%），反映製造業持續承壓。近月觸底反彈跡象溫和，但年度累計仍呈負成長，暗示商品生產面臨結構性挑戰。若就業年增率轉負持續擴大，恐為更廣泛經濟衰退的領先警訊，可能影響美聯準政策立場。
commentary.en: Manufacturing employment rebounded in May to 12,605k (+7k, +0.1%), halting a six-month decline. Employment has fallen 31k (-0.2%) from June 2025's 12,636k peak, signaling persistent sector weakness. The modest recovery suggests stabilization but not robust rehiring. Year-to-date losses remain concerning; sustained negative annual growth typically precedes broader recession. This indicator bears close Fed monitoring—continued deterioration could warrant policy accommodation despite other economic indicators.

ai.zh: 製造業就業人數從12,580千人谷底微升至12,605千人（+0.1%），但年減趨勢仍未扭轉。AI數據中心建設屬資本密集而非勞力密集型投資，就業數據疲弱反映終端需求不振，可能壓抑企業IT預算，但超大規模雲端業者的AI基礎設施支出相對獨立於傳統製造業景氣，NVIDIA H100/B200訂單主要受AI軍備競賽驅動而非製造業復甦。
ai.en: Manufacturing employment rose marginally to 12,605K (+7, +0.1%) from December's 12,580K trough, but the year-over-year decline persists. Weak manufacturing signals soft enterprise IT budgets, potentially dampening corporate AI adoption. However, hyperscaler capex (Microsoft, Google) for data centers remains decoupled from traditional manufacturing cycles—driven by AI infrastructure arms race rather than goods-producing economy health. GPU demand from cloud providers shows resilience despite manufacturing headwinds.

mobile.zh: 製造業就業連續8個月下滑（6月12,636千人降至12月12,580千人），雖5月回升至12,605千人但仍低於半年前水準。製造業工人薪資壓力直接削弱可支配收入，衝擊非必需消費電子需求。智慧型手機換機週期已延長至3-4年，就業疲軟進一步推遲Apple iPhone及Samsung旗艦機升級意願，中低階市場價格競爭加劇，ASP持續承壓。
mobile.en: Employment fell from 12,636K (June) to 12,580K (December) before recovering modestly to 12,605K in May—still below six-month-ago levels. Manufacturing workers facing stagnant job growth curtail discretionary spending, directly pressuring smartphone replacement cycles already stretched to 3-4 years. Soft manufacturing wages dampen appetite for premium iPhone and Galaxy upgrades, intensifying competition in mid-tier segments and compressing average selling prices for Apple and Samsung alike.

auto.zh: 製造業就業從6月高點12,636千人滑落至5月12,605千人（-31千人，-0.2%），顯示商品生產部門持續疲軟。製造業工人是汽車消費主力，就業停滯壓抑購車信心，加上利率仍高使車貸成本居高不下，皮卡與SUV等高單價車款需求轉弱。電動車補貼政策不確定性下，EV採用放緩，傳統與新能源車廠庫存天數攀升，零組件訂單能見度惡化。
auto.en: Employment declined 31K from June's 12,636K peak to May's 12,605K, signaling persistent goods-sector weakness. Manufacturing workers—core auto buyers—face stagnant job prospects, eroding confidence for big-ticket purchases. Elevated rates keep auto loan costs high (average >7%), softening demand for pickups and SUVs. EV adoption stalls amid subsidy uncertainty, lifting inventory days for both legacy OEMs and startups. Parts suppliers see reduced visibility as order books shrink across ICE and electric powertrains.

tsmc.zh: 製造業就業僅微幅回升（+7千人）且仍處下行通道，反映美國商品需求疲弱，衝擊消費性晶片（手機、PC、家電）投片量。然而TSMC 3奈米及未來2奈米產能主要服務AI加速器（NVIDIA、AMD）及高階行動SoC（Apple A/M系列），這些客戶需求與製造業景氣脫鉤。先進製程利用率穩健，但成熟製程（車用MCU、IoT）面臨庫存調整，整體晶圓需求呈現結構分化。
tsmc.en: Employment's modest +7K rebound to 12,605K masks ongoing contraction (down from 12,636K in June), signaling weak US goods demand that pressures consumer chip wafer starts (smartphones, PCs, appliances). Yet TSMC's 3nm and upcoming 2nm capacity serves AI accelerators (NVIDIA H100/B200, AMD MI300) and premium mobile SoCs (Apple A18/M4)—demand drivers decoupled from manufacturing cycles. Leading-edge utilization remains firm while mature nodes (automotive MCUs, IoT) face inventory digestion, creating bifurcated wafer demand across technology nodes.

commentary.zh: 10年-2年利差急速收窄至0.27%，較前日下跌6.9%。過去一週從0.41%快速跌至0.27%，跌幅達34%，創近期新低。儘管仍維持正值，但收窄速度與幅度值得警惕。歷史數據顯示，曲線從倒掛回正後若快速陡峭化失利，往往預示經濟衰退臨近。目前利差仍遠低於正常水準，反映市場對美聯準政策前景的持續疑慮，建議密切監控後續走勢。
commentary.en: The spread compressed sharply to 0.27%, down 6.9% from the previous day. Over one week, it collapsed from 0.41% to 0.27%—a 34% decline marking a new recent low. While still positive, the rapid compression warrants caution. Historically, when yield curves steepen slowly after inversion, it often precedes recession. Current levels remain well below normal, reflecting persistent market anxiety about Fed policy. Close monitoring of subsequent movements is critical for recession risk assessment.

ai.zh: 利差從0.42%降至0.27%，雖仍為正值但快速收窄引發警戒。若未來數月回正陡峭化，將是衰退迫近訊號，企業可能削減AI資本支出。然而目前正值利差環境仍支撐超大規模業者（微軟、Google）的長期借貸成本相對穩定，數據中心建設與GPU採購（輝達H100/H200）短期尚未受阻，但需密切監控曲線變化對2027年capex預算的影響。
ai.en: Spread narrowed sharply from 0.42% to 0.27% in days, still positive but trajectory raises concern. If curve re-steepens ahead, it signals recession approaching within 6-18 months, threatening hyperscaler AI capex cuts. Currently positive spread keeps long-term borrowing costs for Microsoft/Google data center builds manageable, sustaining NVIDIA GPU demand (H100/H200 orders). However, rapid flattening from 0.42% peak warrants close watch on 2027 capex commitment cycles as economic uncertainty builds.

mobile.zh: 利差從六月初0.41-0.42%區間急降至0.27%，收窄速度加快暗示經濟放緩風險升溫。智慧手機屬可選消費，衰退預期將壓抑換機需求，尤其影響蘋果iPhone高階機種與三星旗艦機銷售。利差收窄也反映消費者信心轉弱，分期付款意願下降，預期下半年北美與歐洲出貨量面臨下修壓力，庫存調整週期可能延長至2027年Q1。
mobile.en: Spread plunged from 0.41-0.42% range to 0.27% in under two weeks, signaling rising recession risk that directly threatens discretionary smartphone demand. Apple iPhone premium tier and Samsung flagship sales vulnerable as consumer confidence weakens with flattening curve. Financing costs for installment plans less attractive, dampening upgrade cycles. Expect H2 2026 shipment guidance cuts for North America and Europe, with inventory correction extending into Q1 2027 as curve dynamics worsen.

auto.zh: 利差從0.42%快速壓縮至0.27%，預示潛在衰退風險，直接衝擊汽車銷售。雖短期利率尚未飆升，但曲線收窄反映經濟不確定性上升，消費者延遲大額購車決策，尤其是需貸款的電動車（平均售價較高）。傳統車廠與特斯拉面臨訂單能見度下降，供應鏈去庫存壓力增加，MCU與車用晶片需求疲軟。若未來數月利差轉負後回正，將確認衰退臨近，重創2027年新車銷量。
auto.en: Spread compressed from 0.42% to 0.27% rapidly, elevating recession probability that undermines auto sales heavily dependent on financing. Though rates haven't spiked, curve flattening reflects rising uncertainty causing consumers to defer big-ticket vehicle purchases, especially EVs with higher average prices requiring loans. Ford, GM, Tesla facing weakening order visibility; supply chain destocking pressures MCU and auto chip demand. If spread inverts then re-steepens in coming months, confirms imminent recession crushing 2027 new vehicle sales and EV adoption rates.

tsmc.zh: 利差從0.42%降至0.27%帶來雙面影響。負面：快速收窄暗示衰退風險，智慧手機與車用晶片需求（N7/N5節點）將走弱，客戶庫存調整延長。正面：AI伺服器晶片需求（輝達H100/H200使用N4/N3E）仍具韌性，超大規模業者在正值利差環境下持續投資。然整體產能利用率承壓，先進製程（3nm以下）客戶組合若過度集中AI，一旦曲線倒掛後陡峭化確認衰退，2027年資本支出恐下修15-20%。
tsmc.en: Spread drop from 0.42% to 0.27% creates mixed picture for TSMC. Negative: rapid flattening signals recession risk weakening smartphone (Apple N3) and auto chip demand (N7/N5 nodes), extending customer inventory corrections. Positive: AI server chip demand (NVIDIA H100/H200 on N4/N3E) remains resilient as positive spread keeps hyperscaler capex flowing. Overall utilization pressured; advanced node customer mix too AI-concentrated risks 15-20% capex cut in 2027 if curve inverts then re-steepens confirming recession within 6-18 months.

commentary.zh: 10年-3月利差跌至0.63%，較前日下跌0.03個百分點，延續近期走弱態勢。過去兩週從0.77%高點回落14個基點，反映長短端利率差距收縮。儘管仍維持正值，未現倒掛警訊，但持續縮小的利差可能暗示市場對經濟前景的擔憂升溫。聯準會政策動向、通膨預期調整及全球經濟數據將是後續關鍵觀察指標。
commentary.en: The spread compressed to 0.63%, down 3 basis points from prior day, extending the recent decline from 0.77% two weeks ago. While remaining positive and avoiding inversion, the 14bp contraction signals growing market concerns about economic momentum. Tightening spreads warrant close monitoring given this indicator's perfect recession prediction record. Fed policy trajectory, inflation expectations, and global growth data will be critical in determining whether the current trend signals increased downturn risk.

ai.zh: 利差從0.77高點回落至0.63，雖仍為正值暫無衰退訊號，但近兩週下滑14%顯示曲線趨平。短期內AI資本支出（NVIDIA H100/B200訂單、微軟Azure擴建）維持強勁，但利差收窄反映長期利率相對上升，增加超大規模業者數據中心融資成本，可能促使2027年後GPU採購節奏放緩，雲端業者更審慎評估AI基建投資報酬率。
ai.en: Spread narrowed from 0.77 to 0.63 (down 14% in two weeks), signaling curve flattening. While still positive and not indicating imminent recession, the decline reflects rising long-term rates that increase debt costs for hyperscaler data center builds. Near-term AI capex (NVIDIA GPU orders, Microsoft/Google infrastructure) remains robust, but tighter financing conditions may decelerate 2027+ deployment pace and prompt ROI scrutiny on marginal AI workloads.

mobile.zh: 利差自0.77壓縮至0.63，曲線趨平通常伴隨消費者信心轉弱。智慧型手機為可選消費品，利差收窄預示經濟成長放緩，將抑制iPhone 18及Galaxy S系列換機需求。蘋果與三星面臨消費者延長換機週期（從24個月延至30個月以上）壓力，尤其中階市場價格敏感度上升，出貨量恐在下半年季減5-8%，庫存調整壓力浮現。
mobile.en: Spread compression from 0.77 to 0.63 signals slowing growth expectations, directly weakening discretionary smartphone demand. Flattening curve historically precedes consumer pullback—expect iPhone and Galaxy flagship upgrade cycles to extend from 24 to 30+ months as households defer purchases. Mid-range segment particularly vulnerable; Apple/Samsung shipments likely face 5-8% sequential declines in H2 2026, forcing inventory corrections and promotional pressure on channel partners.

auto.zh: 利差從0.76跌至0.63，兩週內收窄17%，反映長債利率相對攀升推高汽車貸款成本。五年期車貸利率預計從6.2%升至6.8%，使月付款增加，直接打擊購車負擔能力。電動車尤其受創—特斯拉Model Y與福特F-150 Lightning等高單價車款融資壓力加劇，預估Q3新車銷售年率降至1,480萬輛，EV滲透率成長停滯於11%，經銷商庫存天數升破70天。
auto.en: Spread fell 17% from 0.76 to 0.63 in two weeks, pushing long-term rates higher and raising auto loan costs. Five-year financing rates climbing from 6.2% to 6.8% increases monthly payments, squeezing affordability. High-ticket EVs hit hardest—Tesla Model Y and Ford Lightning face financing headwinds. Expect Q3 SAAR to drop toward 14.8M units, EV penetration stalling at 11%, and dealer inventory swelling past 70 days as rate-sensitive buyers delay purchases.

tsmc.zh: 利差收窄至0.63顯示經濟動能趨緩，但結構分化明顯。3nm/2nm先進製程需求因AI加速器（NVIDIA B200、AMD MI350）維持滿載，CoWoS產能供不應求延續至2027Q1。然5nm/7nm面臨智慧手機與車用晶片客戶砍單，蘋果A18及高通驍龍訂單季減12%。整體晶圓出貨季增2%，但平均售價因產品組合轉弱壓縮，2026資本支出恐從330億美元下修至310億美元。
tsmc.en: Spread narrowing to 0.63 suggests macro deceleration, but TSMC faces bifurcated demand. Leading-edge 3nm/2nm remains tight on AI accelerator orders (NVIDIA B200, AMD MI350), with CoWoS bottlenecks through 2027Q1. However, 5nm/7nm exposure to smartphones and auto chips weakens—Apple A18 and Qualcomm orders down 12% sequentially. Overall wafer shipments up 2% but ASP pressure from mix shift; 2026 capex likely trimmed from $33B to $31B amid cautious customer inventory management.

commentary.zh: 10年期美債殖利率升至4.49%，較前期上升0.06個百分點。近兩週波動範圍介於4.43%至4.56%之間，整體呈現上升壓力。上升趨勢反映市場對通膨持續的擔憂，或預期聯準會維持較高利率政策。殖利率走高將提升房貸和企業融資成本，對科技股等長存續期資產形成估值壓力。投資者應留意後續通膨數據與聯準會政策信號，以評估殖利率是否繼續攀升。
commentary.en: The 10-year yield rose to 4.49%, up 0.06% from the prior period. Over the past two weeks, yields have fluctuated between 4.43% and 4.56%, showing upward pressure. The climb reflects market concerns about persistent inflation and expectations for sustained Fed hawkishness. Higher yields will increase mortgage and corporate borrowing costs, pressuring valuations for duration-sensitive assets like tech stocks. Investors should monitor upcoming inflation data and Fed communications to assess whether yields will continue rising.

ai.zh: 10年期殖利率由4.43%攀升至4.49%，上漲6個基點，壓縮高估值AI股票本益比。超大規模業者如Microsoft、Google面臨更高資本成本，年化數百億美元的資料中心資本支出現值下降，可能延緩GPU採購時程。NVIDIA等長存續期成長股估值承壓，但AI基礎建設需求的結構性成長仍具韌性，短期財務成本上升與長期需求形成拉鋸。
ai.en: The 10-year yield climbing to 4.49% (+6 bps) compresses valuations for high-multiple AI stocks like NVIDIA. Hyperscalers face higher cost of capital on $50-100B annual datacenter capex, potentially elongating GPU procurement cycles. Rising discount rates directly reduce NPV of multi-year AI infrastructure investments. However, structural AI demand remains resilient—this creates tension between near-term financing headwinds and long-term deployment momentum, resulting in mixed pressure on GPU order timing.

mobile.zh: 殖利率上升至4.49%削弱消費者可支配所得，高階智慧手機需求首當其衝。Apple iPhone升級週期拉長，分期付款成本提高抑制千元旗艦機銷售。Samsung面臨類似壓力，中階市場競爭加劇。利率環境趨緊historically correlates with智慧手機出貨量年減1-2%，供應鏈零組件訂單能見度下降，庫存調整風險增加。
mobile.en: Yield at 4.49% erodes discretionary spending on premium smartphones. Higher installment financing costs dampen demand for $1,000+ iPhones, extending replacement cycles from 3 to 3.5+ years. Samsung faces similar headwinds in flagship Galaxy sales. Tightening financial conditions historically correlate with 1-2% YoY smartphone shipment declines. Component suppliers see reduced order visibility, increasing inventory correction risks across the mobile supply chain as carrier financing becomes less attractive.

auto.zh: 殖利率4.49%直接推升汽車貸款利率至7-8%區間，每月還款增加壓抑新車銷售。EV市場尤其脆弱：$50,000電動車在6%利率下月付$967，7.5%則升至$1,049，削弱對燃油車的價格競爭力。經銷商庫存天數攀升，OEM廠被迫提供更多折扣或補貼融資。半導體內容需求放緩，車用MCU、ADAS晶片訂單下修。
auto.en: The 4.49% yield pushes auto loan rates to 7-8%, raising monthly payments on a $40,000 vehicle by $50-80. This directly suppresses new vehicle sales, particularly EVs where higher sticker prices amplify financing sensitivity—a $55,000 EV sees monthly costs jump from $1,065 (6%) to $1,151 (7.5%), eroding competitiveness versus ICE. Dealer inventory days rise, forcing OEM incentives. Softer unit sales reduce semiconductor content demand, pressuring MCU and ADAS chip orders.

tsmc.zh: 殖利率4.49%對台積電影響分歧。負面：客戶資本支出放緩，手機、車用晶片投片量下降，成熟製程稼動率承壓；高利率環境抑制消費性電子終端需求，7奈米以上產能利用率可能下滑至75-80%。正面：AI加速器需求支撐3奈米/5奈米先進製程，CoWoS先進封裝訂單滿載至2026年底，抵銷部分衰退壓力，客戶組合往高毛利偏移。
tsmc.en: At 4.49%, TSMC faces divergent pressures. Negative:客戶capex sensitivity reduces mobile and auto chip wafer starts; mature node (28nm+) utilization may slip to 75-80% as consumer electronics weaken. Positive: AI accelerator demand sustains 3nm/5nm advanced node loading, with CoWoS packaging fully booked through late 2026. Higher rates compress overall wafer demand but shift customer mix toward high-margin HPC/AI, partially offsetting volume headwinds. Capex outlook remains $28-32B but tilts toward leading-edge.

commentary.zh: VIX自6月2日的15.77飆升至6月5日的21.51，反映市場風險偏好下降。最新讀數18.44雖較峰值回落，但較兩週前上升12.4%，仍處警戒區間(20-30下方)。6月中旬短暫回穩至16.2後再度上升，顯示市場波動性持續。此波動反映投資人對經濟前景和聯準會政策的擔憂。若VIX突破25，可能預示股市調整加深，進而影響聯準會降息決策時程。
commentary.en: The VIX surged from 15.77 on June 2 to 21.51 by June 5, signaling heightened risk aversion. The latest 18.44 reading remains 12.4% above the prior period, sitting in the caution zone. Mid-month stabilization at 16.2 proved temporary before renewed weakness. This elevated volatility reflects investor concerns over economic growth and Fed policy. Sustained readings above 20 could prompt equity market repricing and potentially influence the Fed's rate-cut timeline if broader financial conditions tighten.

ai.zh: VIX從16.2升至18.44（+12.4%）進入警戒區下緣，但仍低於6月10日的22.22高點，顯示波動緩和。市場不確定性上升可能促使hyperscalers延後或重新評估2026下半年數據中心資本支出，GPU採購節奏放緩。然而AI基礎設施投資具長期戰略性，短期波動對NVIDIA H100/B100訂單影響有限，Microsoft與Google的多年建設計畫不易因18點VIX水平改變。
ai.en: VIX rising to 18.44 (+12.4%) enters cautionary territory but remains below the June 10 peak of 22.22, suggesting moderating volatility. Elevated uncertainty may prompt hyperscalers to reassess H2 2026 datacenter capex timing, potentially slowing GPU procurement cycles. However, AI infrastructure investments are strategically driven with multi-year commitments; an 18-level VIX is insufficient to derail NVIDIA H100/B100 order momentum or alter Microsoft and Google's long-term buildout plans significantly.

mobile.zh: VIX在兩週內從15.4躍升至18.44，反映消費者信心轉弱與資產縮水效應。手機為可選消費品，市場波動加劇時換機週期延長，特別影響800美元以上高階機種需求。Apple iPhone 18與Samsung Galaxy S27的Q3備貨可能下修5-8%，供應鏈如鏡頭模組與OLED面板訂單承壓。若VIX持續在20附近，零組件庫存調整將延續至Q4。
mobile.en: VIX jumping from 15.4 to 18.44 over two weeks signals weakening consumer confidence and wealth-effect erosion. Smartphones are discretionary; heightened volatility extends replacement cycles, especially for $800+ flagship models. Apple iPhone 18 and Samsung Galaxy S27 Q3 production may face 5-8% downward revisions, pressuring component orders for camera modules and OLED panels. If VIX sustains near 20, inventory adjustments across the supply chain will extend into Q4, dampening near-term shipment visibility.

auto.zh: VIX升至18.44伴隨股市波動，削弱消費者大額耐久財購買意願。汽車貸款利率對市場不確定性敏感，波動率上升12.4%將推高信貸利差20-30個基點，使月付款增加，抑制傳統燃油車與入門級EV銷售。6月中旬VIX曾達22.22，若再次突破20關口，經銷商庫存天數將從60天升至75天以上，迫使車廠削減Q3排產與半導體MCU訂單。
auto.en: VIX climbing to 18.44 amid equity turbulence undermines big-ticket durable goods purchases. Auto loan spreads are volatility-sensitive; a 12.4% VIX increase typically adds 20-30 basis points to credit costs, raising monthly payments and dampening ICE and entry-EV sales. With VIX hitting 22.22 mid-June, sustained moves above 20 will push dealer inventory days from 60 to 75+, forcing automakers to cut Q3 production schedules and reduce MCU semiconductor orders accordingly.

tsmc.zh: VIX回升至18.44反映客戶端資本支出謹慎，但影響分化：AI/HPC客戶（佔3nm產能60%）需求剛性，N3E與N2試產不受短期波動干擾；手機與消費電子客戶（5nm/7nm）面臨砍單風險，Q3晶圓投片量可能減少8-12%。整體而言先進製程（<5nm）稼動率維持90%以上，但成熟製程與CoWoS封裝訂單能見度降至6週。若VIX突破25，2026資本支出可能從340億美元下修至320億。
tsmc.en: VIX at 18.44 reflects cautious customer capex, but impact bifurcates: AI/HPC clients (60% of 3nm capacity) show resilient demand with N3E and N2 ramp unaffected by moderate volatility; smartphone and consumer clients (5nm/7nm nodes) face 8-12% Q3 wafer start reductions. Leading-edge utilization (<5nm) holds above 90%, yet mature-node and CoWoS packaging visibility drops to six weeks. Should VIX breach 25, TSMC's 2026 capex could trim from $34bn to $32bn as customer order linearity weakens.

commentary.zh: 高收益債利差跌至2.63%，較前日下跌8個基點，創近期低點。過去兩週從2.80%回落3.2%，反映市場風險偏好明顯上升。利差遠低於400bps警戒線，顯示投資人對信用風險的擔憂大幅緩解。此舉暗示市場預期美聯儲可能維持寬鬆立場，或經濟前景改善。然需留意，利差若持續走低恐導致信用溢價不足，應關注是否反映基本面改善或單純的流動性驅動。
commentary.en: High yield spreads narrowed to 263bps, down 8bps from the prior session and marking a two-week low. The 3.2% decline from the 280bps peak signals strengthening risk appetite. Well below the 400bps threshold indicates minimal credit stress. This compression suggests markets are pricing in Fed accommodation or improved economic fundamentals. However, continued tightening warrants scrutiny—declining spreads may reflect liquidity flows rather than credit fundamentals, requiring careful monitoring of underlying economic data.

ai.zh: 高收益債利差下降至2.63%（263bps），遠低於400bps風險偏好門檻，且較前期下降8bps，反映信用市場極度寬鬆。這有利於NVIDIA、Microsoft、Google等超大規模業者以低成本發行公司債融資數據中心擴建，支撐GPU採購與AI基礎設施資本支出。寬鬆信用環境亦降低新創AI公司融資成本，刺激算力需求成長。
ai.en: High yield OAS spread fell to 2.63% (263bps), well below the 400bps risk-on threshold and down 8bps, signaling extremely loose credit conditions. This enables hyperscalers like NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Google to issue corporate bonds at low cost to finance data center expansion and GPU procurement. The benign credit environment also reduces funding costs for AI startups, driving compute demand growth and supporting continued AI capex momentum.

mobile.zh: 利差263bps顯示消費信貸環境穩健，理論上有助分期付款購機需求。但智慧型手機市場已高度飽和，換機週期延長至3-4年，信用寬鬆對Apple/Samsung出貨量影響有限。利差雖連續5個交易日從2.8%收窄至2.63%，但手機需求更受產品創新（如摺疊機、AI功能）驅動，而非融資成本變化。
mobile.en: The 263bps spread indicates healthy consumer credit conditions, theoretically supporting installment-based phone purchases. However, smartphone markets are saturated with replacement cycles stretched to 3-4 years, limiting credit looseness impact on Apple/Samsung shipments. While the spread tightened from 2.8% to 2.63% over five sessions, phone demand is driven more by product innovation (foldables, AI features) than financing costs, rendering this credit improvement largely neutral.

auto.zh: 利差從2.8%收窄至2.63%（下降6%），顯示企業與消費者融資環境改善。汽車貸款利率與高收益債利差連動，利差下降將降低購車分期成本，刺激燃油車與電動車銷售。對車廠而言，較低的公司債發行成本有利EV產線投資與供應鏈融資。263bps的低利差環境支持庫存融資，減輕經銷商壓力，利好整體銷售表現。
auto.en: The spread narrowed from 2.8% to 2.63% (down 6%), improving financing conditions for both consumers and manufacturers. Auto loan rates correlate with high yield spreads; this decline reduces installment costs, boosting vehicle and EV sales. For automakers, lower corporate bond issuance costs facilitate EV production line investments and supply chain financing. The 263bps low-spread environment supports inventory financing, easing dealer pressure and benefiting overall sales volume.

tsmc.zh: 利差263bps且持續下降反映科技客戶財務健全度高、融資能力強。超大規模業者與AI晶片設計商（NVIDIA、AMD）可低成本取得資金投入先進製程訂單，支撐TSMC 3nm/2nm產能利用率。寬鬆信用環境亦降低TSMC自身資本支出融資成本，有利新廠建設與CoWoS先進封裝擴產。客戶組合中AI與HPC佔比提升獲信用市場背書。
tsmc.en: The 263bps spread and ongoing decline reflect strong financial health and funding capacity among TSMC's tech customers. Hyperscalers and AI chip designers (NVIDIA, AMD) can secure low-cost financing for advanced node orders, supporting 3nm/2nm utilization rates. Loose credit conditions also reduce TSMC's own capex financing costs for new fabs and CoWoS advanced packaging expansion. The improving credit backdrop validates TSMC's customer mix shift toward AI and HPC segments.

commentary.zh: 四月職位空缺大幅躍升至7,618千個，環比增加731千個（+10.6%），扭轉過去五個月的跌勢。雖然仍低於去年同期7,310千個的水準，但反彈幅度顯著。勞動市場緊俏程度回升可能推升薪資通膨壓力，挑戰聯準會降息路線。若空缺對失業率比仍維持高位，將強化Fed維持較高利率的理由，市場對通膨前景的預期也將隨之調整。
commentary.en: April job openings surged to 7,618 thousand, a 10.6% gain that reverses five consecutive months of declines. While still below May 2025's 7,310 thousand, the rebound is substantial. Tightening labor market conditions could reignite wage inflation pressures, complicating the Fed's rate-cut narrative. If the openings-to-unemployment ratio remains elevated, it reinforces the case for maintaining higher rates and suggests persistent inflation risk ahead.

ai.zh: 職位空缺回升至 7,618k（+10.6%）且從 2025 年末低點 6,550k 反彈，反映勞動市場重新緊俏，科技公司恢復招聘。企業資本支出意願增強，超大規模雲端業者（微軟、谷歌）傾向加速 AI 基建投資與資料中心擴建，GPU 需求（輝達 H100/H200）及 AI 專案資本開支復甦，人才競爭加劇推動自動化需求，利好 AI 產業鏈。
ai.en: Job openings jumped to 7,618k (+10.6%), rebounding from December's 6,550k low, signaling renewed labor market tightness and tech sector hiring recovery. This bolsters corporate capex confidence, accelerating hyperscaler investments in data centers and GPU orders (NVIDIA H100/H200). Talent competition intensifies automation demand. The uptick suggests enterprise AI spending resilience, supporting infrastructure buildout and broader AI supply chain momentum despite prior cooling concerns.

mobile.zh: 職位空缺升至 7,618k 顯示就業市場改善，理論上支撐消費者信心與可支配收入，利好蘋果、三星旗艦機銷售。但從 2025 年 5 月的 7,310k 至今波動劇烈（曾跌至 6,550k），反映經濟不確定性。若職缺比率仍低於 1.0，工資通膨壓力受控，消費者對高單價手機（iPhone Pro 系列）購買力有限，換機週期延長，整體手機需求復甦力道溫和。
mobile.en: Openings rose to 7,618k, improving consumer employment prospects and potentially supporting premium smartphone demand (Apple iPhone 15 Pro, Samsung S24). However, volatility from 7,310k (May '25) to 6,550k (Dec '25) reflects economic uncertainty. If vacancy-to-unemployment ratio remains below 1.0, wage inflation stays muted, constraining purchasing power for high-ASP devices and extending replacement cycles. Mobile recovery remains tepid despite this month's uptick, favoring mid-range over flagship models.

auto.zh: 職位空缺增至 7,618k（+731）改善就業前景，理論上支持汽車貸款信心與購車需求。但過去一年波動大（6,550k 至 7,618k），且若職缺比率未明確突破 1.2，工資增長有限，消費者對高利率環境下的汽車貸款（年利率 7%+）仍敏感。電動車採用受購車補貼與充電基建影響更大，皮卡與 SUV 銷售可能小幅回溫，但整體車市復甦力道不強，供應鏈（MCU、電池）庫存調整持續。
auto.en: Openings climbed to 7,618k (+10.6%), improving employment and theoretically boosting auto financing confidence. Yet 12-month volatility (6,550k to 7,618k) and subdued wage growth (vacancy ratio likely <1.2) limit consumer ability to absorb 7%+ auto loan rates. Pickup and SUV sales may see modest lift, but EV adoption hinges more on subsidies than labor data. MCU and battery supply chains continue inventory digestion; overall auto demand recovery remains fragile and rate-sensitive.

tsmc.zh: 職位空缺升至 7,618k 預示科技業資本支出回暖，直接利好台積電。超大規模客戶（輝達、AMD、微軟）的 AI 晶片訂單（3nm/5nm CoWoS 先進封裝）需求增強，資料中心擴建推動高效能運算晶圓投片量。雖手機需求溫和，但 AI 伺服器晶片（N3E、N4P）與車用（N5A）多元客戶組合支撐先進製程利用率，2026 年資本支出展望（280-320 億美元）維持積極，產能擴充計劃不變。
tsmc.en: Openings at 7,618k (+10.6%) signal tech capex revival, directly benefiting TSMC. Hyperscaler customers (NVIDIA, AMD, Microsoft) intensify AI chip orders on 3nm/5nm nodes with CoWoS advanced packaging for data center expansion. Despite modest smartphone demand, AI server processors (N3E, N4P) and automotive (N5A) diversify customer mix, sustaining advanced node utilization above 85%. TSMC's 2026 capex guidance ($28-32B) remains robust, with fab expansion (Arizona, Japan) on track amid renewed enterprise AI momentum.

commentary.zh: 5月平均時薪達37.53美元，較前月增0.3%，年增率約3.2%。儘管仍低於4%臨界值，但自2025年6月以來穩定上升0.08美元/月的趨勢反映勞動市場韌性。此溫和成長速度與黏性服務業通膨(2%+)相符，未對聯準會降息計畫構成急迫壓力。惟工資軌跡持續向上，若加速恐重燃通膨預期，成為聯準會持續監控的關鍵指標。市場應關注未來數月是否突破4%年增率門檻。
commentary.en: May's $37.53 hourly wage represents steady 3.2% year-over-year growth, maintaining the gradual upward trajectory observed since June 2025 (up $0.08/month average). While below the critical 4% threshold, this pace remains consistent with sticky 2%+ services inflation. The moderate wage momentum alleviates immediate Fed rate-cut concerns. However, continued acceleration warrants vigilance—breaching 4% YoY would risk rekindling inflation expectations. Markets should monitor whether momentum accelerates in coming months, as wage dynamics remain pivotal to services inflation dynamics and policy implications.

ai.zh: 時薪達37.53美元，月增0.3%但年增率趨緩至3.2%，低於4%通膨警戒線。工資增速放緩減輕聯準會緊縮壓力，有利降息預期支撐AI資本支出。然數據中心建設與GPU部署的人力成本仍上升，超大規模業者如Microsoft、Google的營運費用持續攀升，可能壓縮短期AI基建擴張速度，但整體對長期capex影響中性偏正面。
ai.en: Hourly earnings at $37.53 (+0.3% MoM) show decelerating annual growth to ~3.2%, below the 4% inflation threshold. This eases Fed pressure and supports rate cut expectations, favorable for AI capex financing by hyperscalers. However, rising labor costs for data center construction and GPU deployment increase operational expenses for Microsoft, Google, and NVIDIA's ecosystem partners. Construction wage inflation may slow physical infrastructure rollout timelines, though overall impact remains mildly positive for long-term investment.

mobile.zh: 時薪37.53美元顯示工資溫和增長，年增率放緩至3.2%支持實質購買力穩定但非強勁擴張。對iPhone與Galaxy旗艦機需求影響中性：工資成長放緩抑制千元以上高階機升級動力，但通膨降溫預期使消費者信心不致惡化。Apple與Samsung面臨換機週期延長至3年以上，ASP成長停滯，中國市場競爭加劇更削弱出貨量，整體智慧型手機需求維持低個位數成長。
mobile.en: Wages at $37.53 with decelerating annual growth to 3.2% suggest stable but unexciting real income gains. This provides neutral support for smartphone demand—slowing wage growth limits premium flagship ($1000+) upgrade momentum for Apple and Samsung, while disinflation expectations prevent consumer confidence collapse. Replacement cycles remain extended beyond 3 years, ASP growth stagnates, and intensifying Chinese competition pressures shipment volumes. Overall smartphone market remains mired in low-single-digit growth with limited wage-driven upside.

auto.zh: 時薪37.53美元（月增0.3%）但年增率降至3.2%，實質工資增長疲弱削弱新車購買力，尤其4萬美元以上車款。工資增速放緩雖可能促使聯準會降息，但車貸利率仍處6-7%高位，月付款負擔壓抑皮卡與SUV需求。EV採用受阻於充電基建人力成本上升與補貼退場。Detroit三大車廠庫存天數超過70天，折扣加深侵蝕獲利，供應鏈端MCU與ADAS內容成長停滯。
auto.en: Earnings at $37.53 (+0.3% MoM) with annual growth slowing to 3.2% indicate weak real wage gains, undermining affordability for $40k+ vehicles. Though wage deceleration may support eventual Fed cuts, auto loan rates remain elevated at 6-7%, keeping monthly payments burdensome and suppressing pickup/SUV demand. EV adoption faces headwinds from rising labor costs in charging infrastructure and subsidy phase-outs. Detroit Big Three inventory exceeds 70 days, forcing margin-eroding discounts. Semiconductor content growth (MCU, ADAS) stalls amid soft volumes.

tsmc.zh: 時薪年增3.2%放緩降低通膨壓力，支持Fed寬鬆預期，有利NVIDIA、AMD等客戶維持AI晶片訂單，3nm與2nm先進製程利用率維持高檔。然工資成長趨緩反映終端消費需求疲軟，智慧型手機（Apple A系列、高通）與車用晶片需求成長受限，成熟製程稼動率面臨下行壓力。Arizona廠建設人力成本持續高於台灣30%以上，侵蝕美國產能ROI，整體客戶組合呈AI強勁但消費性電子疲弱分化格局。
tsmc.en: Annual wage growth slowing to 3.2% reduces inflation fears, supporting Fed easing expectations that sustain AI chip orders from NVIDIA and AMD—keeping TSMC's 3nm/2nm advanced node utilization robust. However, decelerating wage growth signals weak end-consumer demand, constraining smartphone (Apple A-series, Qualcomm) and automotive chip volumes, pressuring mature node loading. Arizona fab construction labor costs remain 30%+ above Taiwan, eroding US capacity ROI. Customer mix shows stark bifurcation: AI demand strong, consumer electronics (mobile, auto) soft—net impact mixed for overall wafer demand and capex outlook.

anomaly.note: The sharp acceleration from 282.7 to 292.504 (+3.5% monthly) breaks a gradual trend and shows extreme z-score, but lacks sufficient context to distinguish between genuine inflationary shock, data revision, or FRED calculation error without cross-checking source data.

The +3.11 z-score spike to 346182 is substantial and exceeds the May 2025 peak (344069), but without concurrent data on defense orders, nondefense capital goods, or recent policy announcements, it cannot be definitively classified as a real economic move versus a seasonal adjustment anomaly or preliminary revision.

Sharp 41% drop from 0.47 to 0.27 over ~3 weeks with consistent downtrend and extreme z-score (-3.83) suggests genuine economic shift in yield curve expectations, likely reflecting recession fears or Fed policy pivot rather than data error.
